notes: Content-ops repo: no typecheck/test/build. Checks are dependency-free Node scripts (no jq/python/prettier/yamllint available). Contract of a check script: print a normalized failure set to stdout (one per line, sorted); exit 0 = ran (stdout is authoritative); exit 3 = could-not-run (fail-open). verify-ratchet.mjs diffs `now - baseline` where baseline lives in .claude/tasks/.baseline-<NNN>/<id>. Behavior decides whether it blocks; event decides when it runs.

checks.normalize: file:line: retired-term hits (strict set: 'Tier N', 'Chapter chair'), sorted
file: rule (missing front-matter key / invalid lane / missing TL;DR), sorted

checks.baseline_observed: red-by-design (YPO draft flagged; held_review — acceptable). Cannot enforce a human sign-off, so it can never be RATCHET.
red (known 404 CTA routes /apply /nominate /newsletter). Live-200 is network + out-of-band, so it stays SMOKE.

green after the 2026-07-08 sweep. High-precision Part-A anti-slop patterns only (throat-clearing, jargon, meta, vague declaratives); Part-B house-voice items (the name-the-decision contrast, three-part lists, adverbs) are QA-owned judgment calls, deliberately not machine-checked. Adapted from stop-slop (Hardik Pandya, MIT). See substrate/anti-slop.md.

checks.cmd: MANUAL: verify each live CTA route returns 200; run the human dual-pass voice review (content-standards.md §7); confirm Court+legal sign-off for any named-network piece.
